mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for at least one month, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates on future corporate earnings. This compression of present values produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This demand destruction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135230.0 thousand as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth above their 3-month moving averages simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 trading days due to improved earnings visibility and economic momentum.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 3-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363806.0 million as of t-1mo
fact:HOUST at 1957.0 thousand as of t-2mo
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns drops below 0.10 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power for cyclical firms. This supply strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.0991 index as of t-2mo
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of months where INDPRO MoM growth exceeds 1%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, the Fed faces conflicting signals between labor market weakness and inflation control. This policy uncertainty produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors demand higher risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
regime_hint: monetary policy dilemma with deteriorating labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to predictive power is less than 0.02 in terms of Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth. This demand strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors extend valuation multiples in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11403.6 billion chained 2017 dollars as of t-6mo
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) shows a 3-month annualized inflation rate above 3.5% while the federal funds rate is below 5.0%, monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This real rate compression produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.9 index as of t-2mo
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy in inflationary environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the real rate signal's predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5% and remains below that threshold for at least 2 consecutive months, tight labor market conditions signal wage pressure and potential earnings margin compression. This labor cost inflation produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margin expectations are revised downward.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage acceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE remains below 4.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
